Comment.value: This script will import subtitles to existing media from a JSON subtitle format.
Example $fileInfo result: file:3_3_quiz_test.en-global.vtt filemac:/Macintosh HD/Users/jensthorsen/Desktop/CMT Tester/TMH filmer & subtitles/content/video/3_3_quiz_test.en-global.vtt
Check if media exist in LP - don't import subtitles to media that doesn't exist!!!!
If media file exist in FAT
Check if imported text id exists already
If text doesn't exist and media exist - create a new record whether mode is new or update
Check if unique text id is used before in the whole CMT

Calculation.Text: "This text is already imported" & ¶ & ¶ & "A text with an identical id is already imported to a Learning Programme in the Funkis Content Management Tool. Import will now stop."

Comment.value: When there's already a text and the import mode is "update"
When there's already a text and the import mode is "new"

Calculation.Text: "This text is already imported" & ¶ & ¶ & "A text with an identical id is already imported to a Learning Programme in the Funkis Content Management Tool. Import will now stop."
$numOfMediaSubtitles & " subtitles texts imported for " & ¶ & $numOfMediaFiles & " media files."
"Sorry but the content in the selected file does not seem to be a valid json object. Please validate the content at https://jsonlint.com/"
"The selected file does not seem correctly named."
"The file should have a suffix and an extension as: " & FAT Title::Original localisation code & ¶ & ¶ & ".json"
